title.text: Setting up the registration process and registration questions

body:0001.text: This article is machine translated from english

body:0002.text: Why is the registration process important?

body:0003.text: A well-designed registration process is key to creating a smooth experience for new users. Keeping the form short, simple, and clear helps boost user satisfaction and reduces drop-offs.

body:0004.text: Before you start building your registration form, ask yourself:

body:0005.text: What demographic data do I need to collect from all users?

body:0006.text: What information is only relevant for certain participants or projects?

body:0007.text: How to trigger the registration flow?

body:0008.text:
From the Homepage
Trigger the registration flow by clicking the Log In or Register button on the homepage.

body:0009.text:
Using a Direct URL
Access the registration screen directly by visiting https://<platform_domain>/sign-up, which will display the registration popup.

body:0010.text:
Before Participating in a Phase
If Email Confirmation or Account Creation is set as a prerequisite for participation, the registration flow will trigger when the user clicks the participation button (e.g., Submit Survey, Submit Idea, Vote, etc.).

body:0011.text:
After Participating in a Phase
If None is selected as a prerequisite to participate, users can participate without registering. After submission, they will be prompted to register to stay informed.

body:0012.text: How to set up the registration process?

body:0013.text: Create a smooth onboarding experience by customising your platform’s registration steps.

body:0014.text: The registration flow consists of three steps:

body:0015.text: User initiates the sign-up form by entering their email address

body:0016.text: User verifies their email address, then logs in successfully

body:0019.text: You can configure demographic questions for users to fill in

body:0023.text: You can a description to inform users via Settings > Registration > Enter Email step

body:0024.text: Below is an example image of the view users will get to see.

body:0025.text: If the user has entered an existing email address they will in a following step be asked to enter their password. If there is no existing email address the registration flow will continue at step 2.

body:0026.text: ℹ️ Note: You can directly link users to the sign-up form by using the following URL: https://<your platform domain>/sign-up

body:0028.text: By default users will be prompted to verify their email address when registering on the platform.

body:0030.text: If you want to disable email verification on your platform, contact support via the chat bubble at the bottom right of your screen

body:0032.text: You can a description to inform users via Settings > Registration > Complete your profile step

body:0033.text: Below is an example image of the view users will get to see.

body:0035.text: After email verification, users can be shown demographic questions. These can be set as optional or required, depending on your needs.

body:0037.text: If enabled, users can select topics and areas they want to receive updates about, you can add these via:

body:0040.text: When users select preferences they will receive email notifications when a project is published/updated that has been tagged with one of their preferences.

body:0041.text: ℹ️ Users can update their follow preferences or unsubscribe anytime via Account Settings > Notifications or Activity > Following.

body:0042.text: How to customize your registration questions?

body:0043.text: Add, remove, or adjust the questions asked during user registration.

body:0046.text: Year of birth: used for age group insights on dashboards

body:0047.text: Place of residence: linked to geographic areas (Areas tab in the Platform Settings), useful for access control and representation analysis

body:0051.text: You can add a description to clarify what you are asking.

body:0053.text: You can add these types of questions:

body:0061.text: How many people live in your household? (numeric)

body:0062.text: ⚠️ For the representativeness dashboard only single select questions can be used.

body:0064.text: Select the question where you want to add options to

body:0069.text: Access Control: Control participation based on user traits

body:0070.text: Group Segmentation: Sort users into smart or standard groups

body:0071.text: Data Consistency: Only registration fields appear across all dashboards and reports

body:0074.text: Only default registration fields can be used in AI analysis

body:0075.text: Only single-choice questions show up in demographic graphs

body:0076.text: "Year of birth" cannot currently be used for grouping survey answers by age

body:0079.text: Participation projects: View demographic participation per project.

body:0080.text: AI analysis and Auto-insights: Segment insights based on default demographic fields.

body:0082.text: What are project level demographic questions?

body:0084.text: By default, new projects inherit platform-level registration questions

body:0086.text: Choose which questions apply to their project

body:0088.text: To modify this, go to the phase settings and check the Phase access and user data section.

body:0089.text: ⚠️ Project managers can select registration questions that are not enabled on the platform level, however, the questions need to be created by a platform admin in the registration section of the platform settings. After this, a project manager can ask this as a specific question for their project.

body:0090.text: To learn more about access rights specifically, read our article: Understanding Access Rights
